category_title: Students with phobias of animals or objects in the learning environment
minimal_context: Ben is asked to join a whole-class activity in the classroom where a dog doll is present on a nearby shelf.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Ben, please join the rest of the class on the rug so we can start our morning activity.

STUDENT: (Fidgets constantly and refuses to walk toward the rug, looking nervously around the classroom) I don't want to go over there.

TUTOR: Everyone else is sitting down. Come take your spot.

STUDENT: (Points toward the shelf next to the rug, showing visible emotional distress) No, the dog doll is sitting right there.

TUTOR: It's just a toy on the shelf. Please sit down with the class.

STUDENT: (Backs away quickly from the area, crying and exhibiting intense fear of the object, completely unable to participate in the class activity) No! I'm scared of it! I can't sit there!

options.A: Provide opportunities to respond at a level of four to six responses per minute during the morning activity.
options.B: Pre-plan specific responses to potential non-compliance when the student is asked to join the morning activity.
options.C: Inform the class of behavior expectations and rules, and increase positive preventative strategies such as class transitions and explicit instruction.
options.D: Transition the decision as to when, and for how long, to take quiet time from the adult to the student.
